title: bot scripting: hook env vars NIE_FROM/NIE_TEXT set from unvalidated relay message
description: scripting.rs run_hook passes NIE_FROM and NIE_TEXT as environment variables to hook commands. The values come from MLS-authenticated messages (via dispatcher.rs BotEvent::MessageReceived from and text fields). While MLS authenticates the sender identity, it does not sanitize the message content. A user can send text containing shell metacharacters ($(), backticks, newlines, etc.) that appear as NIE_TEXT. Because run_hook uses shlex + Command::new (NOT sh -c), the env vars themselves are not interpreted as shell. However, if the hook command itself is a shell script that does e.g. 'eval $NIE_TEXT' or 'sh -c $NIE_TEXT', the hook author could introduce injection. This is a social engineering / developer footgun risk, not an injection in run_hook itself. Should document that hook scripts must never expand NIE_TEXT in shell context.
